title: [LowerToHW] Lower NonLocalAnchor symbols to hw.innerNameRef in verbatim op
body:
The `NonLocalAnchor` can be used for specifying the symbol name in the `VerbatimOp`. 
This PR lowers the `nla` symbol to the corresponding `hw.innerNameRef`. 
For example, the `@nla1` should be lowered to the wire symbol name.
```
  hw.module @dutModule2() {
    %a = sv.wire sym @__dutModule2__a  : !hw.inout<i3>
 }
 firrtl.nla @nla1 [@dutModule, @dutModule2] ["m", "a"]
 sv.verbatim "[name : {{0}}]" {symbols = [@nla1]}
```
Should be lowered to:
```
sv.verbatim "[name : {{0}}]"  symbols = [#hw.innerNameRef<@dutModule2::@__dutModule2__a>]}
```

This requires building a map of `FIRRTL` operation name to the `hw.innerNameRef` for every module.
After all the modules are lowered, the `nla` op is mapped to the corresponding lowered symbol names.
Finally, the `nla` symbol is substituted with the `hw.innerNameRef` corresponding to the leaf of the instance path.

As a future extension, a hierarchical name can be substituted instead of just the leaf symbol name.
